projectName: MASH E-Commerce - Phone Verification & Two-Factor Authentication
description: Implement phone number verification with OTP and two-factor authentication (2FA) for enhanced account security using Twilio SMS service

successMetrics.security: No plaintext OTP storage, rate limiting on all endpoints
successMetrics.build: npm run build passes with zero errors
successMetrics.tests: All test suites pass with 0 failures

stories.title: Twilio SMS Service Integration
acceptanceCriteria: Twilio account set up with verified Sender ID for Philippines
Created src/lib/sms/twilio.ts service wrapper with TypeScript types
Implemented sendOTP(phoneNumber: string, code: string): Promise<boolean>
Rate limiting: Max 3 OTP sends per phone per 15 minutes
Rate limiting: Max 5 OTP sends per user per hour
Environment variables added: TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER
Tested SMS delivery to Philippine mobile numbers (+63 format)
Error handling for Twilio API failures with retry logic
Logging for SMS send attempts (success/failure tracking)
Cost monitoring: Log SMS count per day for billing alerts

testResults.coverage: Configuration (4), SMS Sending (4), Retry Logic (3), Rate Limiting (4), OTP Formatting (2)

stories.technicalNotes: Use Twilio Programmable SMS API v2010. Test credentials available for non-production. Philippine SMS costs ~$0.0445/message. Use exponential backoff for retry (1s, 3s, 9s). Store rate limit counters in Firestore with TTL.

stories.title: Backend OTP Service API Endpoints (Frontend API Client)
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/lib/api/otp.ts API client with typed functions
Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/otp/send - Generate and send OTP via Twilio
Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/otp/verify - Verify OTP code with hashed comparison
Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/otp/resend - Resend OTP with 60s cooldown
OTP format: 6-digit numeric code (100000-999999)
OTP expiry: 5 minutes from generation
OTP storage: Firestore otp_verifications collection with hashed codes
Responds with { success: boolean, message: string, data?: any }
Authentication required: All endpoints check auth-token cookie
Validation: Phone number must be E.164 format (+63XXXXXXXXXX)

testResults.coverage: Send OTP (6), Verify OTP (6), Resend OTP (6), Integration (5)

stories.technicalNotes: FRONTEND API CLIENT ONLY - Backend implementation required in MASH-Backend repo. Use crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999) for OTP generation. Hash with bcrypt (cost factor 10). Store attemptCount and lock after 3 failed verifications. Clean up expired OTPs with Firestore TTL policy.

stories.title: Firestore Schema & User Profile Updates
acceptanceCriteria: Created Firestore collection otp_verifications with schema definition
OTP document structure: id, userId, phoneNumber, code (hashed), purpose, attempts, expiresAt, verified, createdAt
TTL policy set: Auto-delete documents after 10 minutes
Security rules: Only authenticated users can read/write their own OTP docs
User profile updated with phoneNumber: string field
User profile updated with phoneVerified: boolean field (default false)
User profile updated with phoneVerifiedAt: Timestamp field (optional)
User profile updated with twoFactorEnabled: boolean field (default false)
User profile updated with twoFactorMethod: 'SMS' field (optional)
Migration script for existing users (add new fields with defaults)
stories.technicalNotes: Firestore TTL requires _expiresAt field. Use FieldValue.serverTimestamp() for createdAt. Purpose enum: PHONE_VERIFICATION | 2FA_LOGIN | PHONE_CHANGE. Max attempts = 3, then lock verification.

stories.title: Phone Number Validation Utilities
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/lib/phone-utils.ts with TypeScript utility functions
Function: formatPhoneNumber(input: string): string - Convert to E.164 format
Function: validatePhilippinePhoneNumber(phone: string): boolean - Validate PH numbers
Function: normalizePhoneNumber(input: string): string - Remove spaces, dashes, parentheses
Function: maskPhoneNumber(phone: string): string - Display as +63 *** *** **34
Support formats: +63XXXXXXXXXX, 09XXXXXXXXX, 63XXXXXXXXXX, 9XXXXXXXXX
All functions handle null/undefined/empty strings gracefully
Unit tests: 100% coverage for all phone utility functions
Validation checks: Length (10-13 chars), starts with valid prefix, all digits after prefix
Error messages: Clear, actionable feedback for invalid formats

testResults.coverage: Null safety (16), Format normalization (8), Format conversion (10), PH validation (16), Masking logic (8), Integration (5)

stories.title: Phone Number Input Component
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/components/profile/PhoneNumberInput.tsx component
Auto-formats user input as they type: +63 912 345 6789
Visual validation indicators: Green checkmark (valid), Red X (invalid)
Country code selector dropdown (default: +63 Philippines)
Loading state with spinner during verification process
Disabled state when verification in progress
Error state with red border and error message below input
Success state with green border after verification
Integrates with React Hook Form (register, errors, control)
Zod schema validation: min 10 digits, max 13 chars, valid PH format
stories.technicalNotes: Use shadcn Input component as base. Add onChange handler that calls formatPhoneNumber(). Use lucide-react icons (CheckCircle2, XCircle, Loader2). Debounce validation by 500ms to avoid excessive re-renders.

stories.title: OTP Verification Modal Component
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/components/profile/OTPVerificationModal.tsx component
6-digit OTP input with auto-focus on first digit
Auto-advance to next input on digit entry
Backspace navigates to previous input and clears digit
Visual timer countdown: 5:00 → 4:59 → ... → 0:00
Timer turns red when < 60 seconds remaining
Resend OTP button (disabled for 60s cooldown after send)
Clear error message for: Invalid code, Expired code, Max attempts reached
Success animation: Green checkmark with fade-in transition
Paste detection: Auto-fill all 6 digits from clipboard
Keyboard shortcuts: Enter to submit, Escape to close modal
Accessible: ARIA labels, screen reader announcements for timer
stories.technicalNotes: Use shadcn Dialog component. OTP input: 6 separate Input components with maxLength=1, type=text (not number for better UX). Use refs for input focus management. Timer: setInterval with 1000ms, clear on unmount. Paste: e.clipboardData.getData('text').

stories.title: Profile Page Phone Verification Integration
acceptanceCriteria: Updated src/app/(user)/profile/my-information/page.tsx with Phone section
Phone Number section displays above Address section
Verification status badges: Unverified (orange), Verified (green), Pending (blue)
Unverified: Show 'Verify Phone' button with Shield icon
Verified: Show phone number (masked) + green checkmark + timestamp
Edit flow: Click Edit → Enter phone → Send OTP → Modal opens → Verify
Security notice: 'We'll send a 6-digit code to verify your number' below input
Loading states during: Send OTP, Verify OTP, Save to backend
Error handling: Toast notifications for all error scenarios
Success feedback: Toast + UI update + close modal automatically
stories.technicalNotes: Use existing Card component structure. Badge colors: orange (bg-orange-100 text-orange-800), green (bg-green-100 text-green-800). Disable Edit button when verification in progress. Auto-refresh user profile from backend after verification.

stories.title: Phone Verification Flow Custom Hook
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/hooks/usePhoneVerification.ts custom hook
State: phoneNumber, setPhoneNumber, isVerified, isLoading, error
Function: sendOTP(): Promise<void> - Call backend /otp/send endpoint
Function: verifyOTP(code: string): Promise<boolean> - Verify with backend
Function: resendOTP(): Promise<void> - Resend with cooldown enforcement
Computed: canResend: boolean (true after 60s cooldown)
Computed: cooldownSeconds: number (countdown from 60 to 0)
Error recovery: Automatic retry (max 3) for network failures
Updates AuthContext: Call updateUserProfile() on successful verification
Cleanup: Clear timers and state on unmount
stories.technicalNotes: Use useState for local state, useCallback for functions. Store lastSentAt timestamp for cooldown. Use useEffect for cooldown timer (decrement every 1s). Handle 429 Too Many Requests with exponential backoff.

stories.title: Two-Factor Authentication Settings UI
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/components/profile/TwoFactorSettings.tsx component
Toggle switch: 'Enable Two-Factor Authentication' with smooth animation
Status badge: Enabled (green) or Disabled (gray)
Requirement tooltip: 'Phone number must be verified first' on hover
Disable flow requires OTP verification for security
Confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to disable 2FA?' before disabling
Security notice: 'Adds an extra layer of security to your account' below toggle
Loading state: Spinner on toggle during API call
Error handling: Revert toggle on failure + show error toast
Success feedback: Toast notification + badge update
stories.technicalNotes: Use shadcn Switch component. Disable toggle if phoneVerified === false. Use AlertDialog for disable confirmation. Store 2FA state in AuthContext and sync with backend.

stories.title: Two-Factor Authentication Login Flow
acceptanceCriteria: Updated src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx for 2FA flow
After successful email/password login, check user.twoFactorEnabled
If true, show OTP modal instead of redirecting to dashboard
Send OTP to user's verified phone number automatically
Verify OTP before completing login and issuing full JWT token
Error handling: Max 3 attempts, then lock account for 15 minutes
Show 'Can't access your phone?' link to recovery flow
Remember this device checkbox (30-day trusted device cookie)
Loading states: Sending OTP, Verifying OTP, Completing login
Security logging: Log all 2FA attempts (success/failure) to Firestore
stories.technicalNotes: Store tempToken in memory (not localStorage) after initial login. Send tempToken with OTP to /auth/2fa/verify. Set trusted device cookie with HttpOnly flag. Use proxy.ts to skip 2FA for trusted devices.

stories.title: Two-Factor Authentication Backend Logic
acceptanceCriteria: Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/enable - Enable 2FA (requires verified phone)
Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/disable - Disable 2FA (requires OTP verification)
Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/verify - Verify OTP during login
Login flow modification: Return { requiresTwoFactor: true, tempToken: string } if 2FA enabled
Temp token: Short-lived JWT (5 minutes expiry), only valid for 2FA verification
Device trust: Generate 30-day trust token after successful 2FA, store in HTTP-only cookie
Validation: Check phoneVerified === true before enabling 2FA
Security: Hash and compare OTP codes using bcrypt
Rate limiting: Max 5 failed 2FA attempts per hour per user
Audit trail: Log all 2FA events to security_events collection
stories.technicalNotes: Use JWT with custom payload { userId, purpose: '2FA_TEMP' } for temp tokens. Trust token payload: { userId, deviceId, purpose: 'TRUSTED_DEVICE' }. Generate deviceId from User-Agent + IP hash (SHA-256).

stories.title: AuthContext Two-Factor Authentication Integration
acceptanceCriteria: Updated src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx with 2FA methods
Added enable2FA(): Promise<void> - Enable 2FA for current user
Added disable2FA(): Promise<void> - Disable 2FA with OTP verification
Added verify2FA(code: string): Promise<void> - Verify OTP during login
Added state: twoFactorEnabled: boolean
Added state: twoFactorMethod?: 'SMS'
Modified signInWithEmailPassword() to handle 2FA flow
Store tempToken in memory (useRef) during 2FA login, never in storage
Clear tempToken after successful 2FA verification
Update user profile after enable/disable 2FA
stories.technicalNotes: Use useRef for tempToken to prevent re-renders. Call backend API via apiRequest() from @/lib/api-client. Update user state with spread operator: setUser({ ...user, twoFactorEnabled: true }).

stories.title: Rate Limiting & Abuse Prevention
acceptanceCriteria: OTP generation limits: Max 3 sends per phone per 15 minutes
OTP generation limits: Max 5 sends per user per hour
OTP verification limits: Max 3 attempts per OTP code
Login limits: Max 5 failed 2FA attempts per hour → 1 hour lock
Login limits: Max 10 failed 2FA attempts per day → account review flag
Exponential backoff for resend: 30s → 60s → 120s
Rate limit storage: Firestore rate_limits collection with TTL
Rate limit response: HTTP 429 with Retry-After header
CAPTCHA: Show reCAPTCHA after 3 failed OTP sends (prevent bots)
Admin dashboard: Flag accounts with suspicious activity (high OTP requests)
stories.technicalNotes: Store rate limit counters as Firestore documents: { key: 'otp:+63...', count: number, windowStart: Timestamp, expiresAt: Timestamp }. Use FieldValue.increment() for atomic updates. CAPTCHA: Google reCAPTCHA v3 (invisible).

stories.title: Phone Number Change Flow with Security
acceptanceCriteria: Phone change requires OTP verification of NEW phone number
Phone change requires current password confirmation
If 2FA enabled, also require OTP verification of OLD phone number
Show confirmation dialog: 'Change phone from +63 *** **34 to +63 *** **56?'
Security email sent to user: 'Your phone number was changed'
Old phone number stored in audit log (phone_change_history collection)
New phone resets phoneVerifiedAt timestamp
2FA remains enabled after phone change (no auto-disable)
Loading states during: Verify new phone, Verify old phone, Update backend
Error handling: Rollback on failure, show detailed error message
stories.technicalNotes: Create src/components/profile/ChangePhoneModal.tsx. Store phone change history: { userId, oldPhone (masked), newPhone (masked), timestamp, ipAddress, userAgent }. Use transaction for atomic update (verify + update user profile).

stories.title: Account Recovery for Lost Phone Access
acceptanceCriteria: Added 'Can't access your phone?' link on 2FA login screen
Recovery flow: Click link → Email verification code sent
Recovery email contains: Verification code + link to recovery page
Recovery page: Enter code + answer security question (past order details)
Security questions: 'What was your last order total?' (± ₱50 tolerance)
High-value accounts (>₱10,000 orders) require admin review
Admin review: Flag in admin dashboard for manual verification
Temporary 2FA disable: 7-day grace period before re-enable required
Backup codes: Generate 10 one-time use codes for future emergencies
Security notification: Email sent when 2FA disabled via recovery
stories.technicalNotes: Create src/app/(auth)/account-recovery/page.tsx. Store backup codes hashed (bcrypt). Security questions: Query last 3 orders from Firestore, random selection. Admin review: Add to pending_reviews collection for admin dashboard.

stories.title: Security Audit Trail & Event Logging
acceptanceCriteria: Created Firestore security_events collection with schema
Event types: PHONE_VERIFIED, PHONE_CHANGED, 2FA_ENABLED, 2FA_DISABLED, 2FA_LOGIN_SUCCESS, 2FA_LOGIN_FAILED
Event data: userId, eventType, ipAddress, userAgent, phoneNumber (masked), success, metadata, createdAt
Log all phone verification attempts (success/failure)
Log all 2FA enable/disable actions
Log all 2FA login attempts (with OTP verification result)
Show recent security events in profile page (last 10 events)
Email notifications for critical events: Phone changed, 2FA disabled, Multiple failed 2FA attempts
Admin dashboard: View security events for all users (with filters)
Retention policy: Keep security events for 90 days (auto-delete after)
stories.technicalNotes: Use FieldValue.serverTimestamp() for createdAt. Get IP from request headers (X-Forwarded-For or remote address). Store User-Agent as is. Mask phone: show last 4 digits only (+63 *** *** **34). TTL policy: _expiresAt = createdAt + 90 days.

stories.title: Unit & Integration Tests for Phone/2FA
acceptanceCriteria: Created src/lib/sms/__tests__/twilio.test.ts (100% coverage)
Created src/lib/__tests__/phone-utils.test.ts (100% coverage)
Created src/hooks/__tests__/usePhoneVerification.test.ts (90% coverage)
Created src/components/profile/__tests__/OTPVerificationModal.test.tsx (85% coverage)
Created src/components/profile/__tests__/PhoneNumberInput.test.tsx (90% coverage)
Created src/components/profile/__tests__/TwoFactorSettings.test.tsx (85% coverage)
Test scenarios: Valid input, Invalid input, Rate limiting, Expired OTP, Max attempts
Mock Twilio API calls (no actual SMS in tests)
Mock Firebase Firestore operations
All tests pass: npm run test
stories.technicalNotes: Use Jest + React Testing Library. Mock Twilio: jest.mock('twilio'). Mock Firestore: Use @firebase/rules-unit-testing. Test async flows with waitFor(). Test error states with expect().toThrow().

stories.title: E2E Testing with Playwright
acceptanceCriteria: Created e2e/tests/phone-verification.spec.ts
Created e2e/tests/2fa-login.spec.ts
Test: Phone verification flow (enter phone → OTP modal → verify → success)
Test: 2FA enable flow (verify phone → toggle 2FA → success)
Test: 2FA login flow (login → OTP modal → verify → dashboard)
Test: Phone change flow (change phone → verify new → verify old → success)
Test: Account recovery flow (lost phone → email code → disable 2FA)
Use Twilio test credentials (no actual SMS sent)
All E2E tests pass: npx playwright test
Screenshot capture on failure for debugging
stories.technicalNotes: Playwright config: headless mode for CI. Twilio test credentials: Use magic phone numbers (+15005550006 for success). Mock Firebase auth state for E2E. Use page.waitForSelector() for loading states.

stories.title: UX Polish & Accessibility Improvements
acceptanceCriteria: ARIA labels on all inputs: aria-label, role attributes
Keyboard navigation: Tab through all inputs, Enter to submit, Escape to close
Screen reader: Announce timer countdown, verification status, errors
Focus management: Auto-focus first input on modal open, trap focus within modal
Auto-paste OTP: Use Web OTP API (navigator.credentials.get({ otp: { transport: ['sms'] } }))
Haptic feedback: Vibration on mobile for success/error (navigator.vibrate())
Smooth animations: Success checkmark fade-in, timer color transition, modal slide-up
Error messages: Clear, actionable solutions (e.g., 'Code expired. Click Resend to get a new code.')
Loading states: Skeleton loaders, spinner animations, disabled buttons with visual feedback
Color contrast: WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for interactive elements)
stories.technicalNotes: Web OTP API: Supported in Chrome/Edge on Android. Add <input autocomplete='one-time-code'>. Animations: Framer Motion or CSS transitions. Test with screen reader (NVDA/JAWS). Use Lighthouse for accessibility audit.

stories.title: Documentation, Feature Flags & Gradual Rollout
acceptanceCriteria: Created .github/PHONE_VERIFICATION_2FA.md technical documentation
Updated progress.txt with implementation patterns
Created user-facing FAQ: 'How to verify phone', 'What is 2FA', 'Lost phone access'
Feature flag: NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_PHONE_VERIFICATION (default: false)
Feature flag: NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_2FA (default: false)
Rollout plan: Day 1 (10% users), Day 2 (50% users), Day 3 (100% users)
Monitoring dashboard: Track OTP delivery rate, 2FA adoption, error rates
Alert setup: Email alerts for: SMS delivery < 95%, Error rate > 5%, High OTP costs
Rollback plan: Document steps to disable features and revert database changes
All documentation reviewed and approved
stories.technicalNotes: Feature flags: Use Next.js env variables, check in components with if (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_2FA === 'true'). Gradual rollout: Use userId hash % 100 to determine user group. Monitoring: Send metrics to Firebase Analytics.

external: Twilio account with Philippine Sender ID
Google reCAPTCHA v3 keys (for abuse prevention)
internal: Firebase Firestore: Collections otp_verifications, security_events, rate_limits

high: SMS delivery failures in Philippines → Mitigation: Email OTP fallback
User lockout during 2FA → Mitigation: Recovery flow with backup codes
medium: Rate limiting bypass → Mitigation: CAPTCHA + IP-based limits
SMS cost overrun → Mitigation: Hard daily limits + fraud detection
low: Phone number change errors → Mitigation: Confirmation step + timer
